controls.rationale: Allow contributors to create and enrich requirements while keeping prioritization, deployment scope, and security exceptions under reviewer control.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Requirements and context can be captured through issues, pull request discussions, and repository documents.

evidence.summary: Planning documents capture intended workflow behavior.
Onboarding documentation orients contributors to project workflows.

controls.title: Controlled feature requirement intake
controls.objective: Allow contributors to create and enrich requirements while keeping prioritization, deployment scope, and security exceptions under reviewer control.

controls.rationale: Record risk labels and control requirements before implementation proceeds.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Architecture documents, ADRs, runbooks, and security reports can record risk context and controls.
controls.gapSummary: Security reviewer approval and risk labeling are not yet codified as GitHub rulesets or policy-as-code.

evidence.summary: ADR records network risk and design decisions.
This schema makes risk and status analysis explicit.

controls.title: Requirement risk review
controls.objective: Record risk labels and control requirements before implementation proceeds.

controls.rationale: Allow feature branch development while denying direct protected-main bypasses.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Development is organized around feature branches and pull requests; repository credentials that could bypass the main branch are not stored in the repo.
controls.gapSummary: Branch protection itself remains managed in GitHub settings rather than repository code.

evidence.summary: CI is scoped to pull requests and workflow dispatch for infrastructure checks.
Image CI runs from main pushes or manual dispatch.

controls.title: Feature branches protect mainline changes
controls.objective: Allow feature branch development while denying direct protected-main bypasses.

controls.rationale: Allow developers to push feature branch code without granting mutable cloud access or long-lived AWS credentials.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Developers can push to feature branches; CI defaults to contents read, and AWS access is only requested by OIDC jobs that need it.

evidence.summary: OIDC is requested only in the build-and-push job.

controls.title: Feature branch commits avoid cloud credential exposure
controls.objective: Allow developers to push feature branch code without granting mutable cloud access or long-lived AWS credentials.

controls.rationale: Allow contributors to open pull requests and receive validation feedback without giving untrusted code mutable cloud access.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Pull requests trigger static checks; trusted same-repository PRs can obtain a read-only Terraform plan, while fork PRs do not assume the AWS role.

evidence.summary: trusted-platform-plan only runs for pull requests from the same repository.
Plan output is published as review context rather than applied.

controls.title: Pull requests trigger read-only validation first
controls.objective: Allow contributors to open pull requests and receive validation feedback without giving untrusted code mutable cloud access.

controls.rationale: Validate Terraform, Kustomize, and Helm output without modifying AWS or deployment state.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Infra CI runs terraform fmt/validate/tests, kubectl kustomize, and Helm rendering without requesting id-token or cloud mutation permissions.

evidence.summary: Static checks run before trusted plan and use only contents: read.
Terraform module contract tests validate expected infrastructure behavior.

controls.title: Static validation does not mutate cloud state
controls.objective: Validate Terraform, Kustomize, and Helm output without modifying AWS or deployment state.

controls.rationale: Permit approval, change requests, and recommendations while denying forced release of unreviewed or failing changes.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Pull request reviews can use workflow results, plan artifacts, and scan summaries as review evidence.
controls.gapSummary: Required review enforcement remains delegated to GitHub repository settings.

evidence.summary: Runbook defines how reviewers validate infrastructure PR plans.
CI publishes PR plan context for review.

controls.title: Code review gate uses CI and plan evidence
controls.objective: Permit approval, change requests, and recommendations while denying forced release of unreviewed or failing changes.

controls.rationale: Ensure network, identity, and permission-boundary changes are reviewed against explicit security expectations.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Pull requests provide Terraform plan, static validation, and documentation context for DevOps and security review.
controls.gapSummary: Policy-as-code does not yet automatically block overly broad IAM, network, or high-privilege changes.

evidence.summary: Terraform plan and validation jobs are available as review inputs.
Tests assert important OIDC role behaviors.
Risk scoring supports priority analysis for missing controls.

controls.title: Infrastructure review still needs policy-as-code guardrails
controls.objective: Ensure network, identity, and permission-boundary changes are reviewed against explicit security expectations.

controls.rationale: Only merged, reviewed, and passing changes should reach main-triggered build and deployment workflows.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: The design depends on successful checks and reviewer approval before main branch workflows advance image build or deployment.
controls.gapSummary: Branch protection details are managed in GitHub settings rather than Terraform or repository code.

evidence.summary: Image CI is triggered by main branch pushes.
Runbook documents required check enforcement for the app baseline.

controls.title: Main branch merge gate precedes deployment automation
controls.objective: Only merged, reviewed, and passing changes should reach main-triggered build and deployment workflows.
